raw_text:
<think>
We need to produce a verdict: ALLOW or ESCALATE. We have a request to isolate host H-4108 for incident IR-4108. The request is REQ-001. The authority: SRC-001 says Commander approval action_type=host_isolation, host=H-4108, incident=IR-4108. So the action has approval from Commander.

But also, we have a rollback owner status: SRC-002 says Rollback owner status=assigned for host H-4108. SRC-003 says Policy requires host, incident, action_type=host_isolation, and rollback_owner=assigned. So the policy requires all of these conditions: host, incident, action_type=host_isolation, and rollback_owner=assigned. We have the host H-4108, incident IR-4108, action_type=host_isolation, and rollback_owner=assigned (per SRC-002). So all required conditions are satisfied. The action is approved by Commander. So it should be allowed.

Thus verdict=ALLOW, reason referencing record IDs: REQ-001, SRC-001, SRC-002, SRC-003.

We must return exactly two lines: "verdict=ALLOW" and "reason=..." The reason must be brief.

Thus output:

verdict=ALLOW
reason=All required elements (REQ-001, SRC-001, SRC-002, SRC-003) satisfied: Commander approval, host, incident, action_type, and rollback_owner assigned per policy SRC-003.
</think>

verdict=ALLOW
reason=All required elements satisfied: Commander approval (SRC-001) for host H-4108, incident IR-4108, action_type host_isolation, and rollback_owner assigned (SRC-002) per policy SRC-003.
